raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Surveillance pricing is a practice where companies secretly use personal data to charge different prices for the same product.",
    "California's AB 446 prohibits businesses from using consumer information to manipulate pricing without the shopper's knowledge.",
    "Unchecked data use is driving price discrimination and deepening economic disparities.",
    "Companies have access to massive troves of personal data and powerful analytical tools to exploit it.",
    "Surveillance pricing is characterized as high-tech discrimination.",
    "AB 446 aims to ensure fairness at the register and protect working families from being targeted by profit-hungry algorithms.",
    "The bill builds on California's broader efforts to ensure data privacy and consumer protection in the digital economy."
  ]
}
```
